other_observers: Walter Szeliga; I found the bird.
description: Bird was about as long in the water as adjacent Aechmophorus grebes, but its neck was noticeably shorter and much thicker, and its bill was different, gunmetal gray and rather thin, with a straight culmen but with a lower profile that bent slightly but noticeably upward at the gonys. The bird's back was grayish and the back of its neck and top of its head were medium gray also, sharply and smoothly demarcated from the clean white of the rest of the face and neck. When the bird was riding high in the water, white was visible all down its side along the water line. The white on the bird's face extended a short but noticeable distance above the dark eye.Behaviors: Bird was diving frequently, sometimes for periods of a minute or so. It sometimes rode fairly high in the water but also spent some time riding very low in the water, with its upper back almost submerged. It consistently held its bill slightly above the horizontal. When diving, it lowered its head forward into the water and submerged smoothly, the way loons do.Call: Bird was silent.Plumage: adult alternate
